records.subject: 2nd grade Phonics\nVocabulary; SPED Student
records.mon: 2 x a week after 4:00 PM but the family is flexible; Support throughout summer and school year
records.nonNeg: Must be certified per program requirements for Texas
records.notes:
Student Background
Parent would like tutoring to start as soon as possible and continue throughout the school year.
Student is currently on grade level. He is a rising 2nd grader
Has an IEP.
Previously received ABA therapy from ages 2–5.
Parent shared that early intervention was very beneficial.
Receives speech therapy.
Is in an inclusive classroom.
Receives support with redirection and maintaining focus.
Academic Needs
Struggles with:
Phonics
Vocabulary
Personality & Interests
Very open and friendly personality.
Happy, social boy who loves making friends.
Shows a lot of empathy.
Has an older brother that assists 
Home Environment
Spanish is spoken at home, but the student doesn't use it.
Tutoring Preferences
Must be a certified teacher
Frequency: Ideally 2 times per week, with the possibility of reducing to once per week depending on progress.
Support throughout summer and school year
Availability: After 4:00 PM but the family is flexible
Format: In-person only. Parent is not interested in virtual tutoring because the student benefits from in-person redirection.
Location: Fort Bend County Library – Sienna Annex.

Student Interests & Motivators
Sprunki
Roblox
YouTube Kids
Minecraft

records.subject: Rising 10th grader; Math
records.mon: 2 X a week; Flexible with time
records.nonNeg: Must be certified per program requirements for Texas
records.notes:
Adam is entering 10th grade and will be attending Excel High School, an online private school.

He needs the most support in Math. He has significant gaps in his Math foundation, so I would like the tutor to first assess his current level and identify where he needs to start before moving forward with his high school Math curriculum.

Adam has ADHD and some learning challenges. He does best with a patient, encouraging teacher who can explain concepts clearly, break lessons into smaller steps, and keep him engaged without making him feel overwhelmed.

He does not need ESL support.

I would like to start with two tutoring sessions per week, and I prefer in-home tutoring.

Adam can be a little shy at first, but once he becomes comfortable with someone, he is friendly and communicates well.

One important thing I would like the teacher to know is that building Adam’s confidence in Math is very important to me. He has struggled with Math for a long time, and I would like someone who can help him strengthen his basic skills while gradually preparing him for his high school coursework.

Adam is currently recovering from surgery, so I would like to coordinate his start date based on his recovery.
